parentQuestion: The blood-brain barrier is the protective mechanism that helps maintain the brai...

scaffolds.q: The brain needs protection from substances circulating in the blood.
scaffolds.explain: The brain is catastrophically sensitive to its chemical environment. Let me show you why this matters: many substances that are perfectly harmless in your bloodstream would trigger seizures, neuronal death, or functional collapse if they reached brain tissue directly. This isn't overcaution — it's physics.

scaffolds.q: Which type of cell forms the walls of blood capillaries?
scaffolds.explain: Endothelial cells line all blood vessels. In the brain, though, these cells are special — Navigate this together: and Study this:: they're sealed together with tight junctions that eliminate the gaps other capillaries have. This transforms them from a leaky fence into a fortress wall.
